Free Text: The area should be regarded as potentially hazardous and has Limited Access to authorised persons only. An area of the concourse dedicated as exhibition space. The hazards associated with this space are dependent on the exhibition. The exhibition sites electrical connection is supplied through an overhead suspended cable run. Work on this carries hazards associated with electrocution and Working at Height.

There are several under platform access points which are believed to open into S & T cable runs, or drains. These could not be lifted therefore any work requiring access should consider Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Clause 2 = Monitoring of access paperwork. If entry required Confined Space procedures to be applied. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

The country end of the Platform island is uncovered and therefore liable to become wet and slippery. Some of the Platform runs beneath a road bridge canopy.The canopy also has various electric lights, cables etc. Introducing potential electrocution hazards. The canopy has regular supports some of which have equipment such as fire alarms, emergency phones etc. Two of the pillars support boxed off areas believed to contain electrical equipment. Ticket booth at the London End of the Platform which contains electrical lighting and heating.

There are 5, on platform entrances to the Subway route, between platforms 11-19. The area should be regarded as potentially hazardous and has Limited Access to authorised persons only. There is extensive electrical cabling and piping within the area introducing electrical hazards, which is fenced off, away from the authorised walkway, between the demarcation lines.
